advantages: Simple, well-understood contracts: LIFO and FIFO map directly to many real-world workflows.
Backed by tiny, fast standard-library implementations (list and collections.deque).
Decouples producers and consumers — perfect for streaming and worker pools.
Easy to reason about: only one end matters for each operation.
disadvantages: A naïve list-based queue is O(n) per dequeue — must use deque.
Plain stacks and queues don't support 'find arbitrary item' efficiently.
Don't preserve priority — pick a heap for that.
Unbounded queues can hide backpressure problems by quietly piling up work.
